topic.name: Tax Code Amendments and Financial Literacy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to various tax credits, financial literacy programs, and economic growth measures.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow payment of home school expenses from Coverdell education savings accounts.
Document 2: Amends the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 to require federal financial supervisory agencies to prescribe regulations establishing minimum standards for financial literacy programs.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit to small businesses for providing qualified continuing financial education to their employees.
Document 3: Makes permanent provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001.
Repeal of the estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an individual taxpayer a new tax credit to compensate for income disparity.
Accelerates the termination date for the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the tax credit for investment in energy property to include qualified offshore wind property.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax credit for qualified fuel cell property.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an estate and gift tax exclusion for real property used for farming or conservation purposes.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny a tax deduction for income attributable to the sale of oil and natural gas.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an exclusion from gross income for income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax deduction for qualified tuition and related expenses.

require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to treat such component as a service for which payment shall be made to the laboratory

topic.name: Higher Education Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments focused on improving financial aid, teacher support, and educational services within the framework of the Higher Education Act.

Caps loan forgiveness at $17,500.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to provide for the use of data from the second preceding tax year
Requires such simplification to include data sharing between the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and the Department of Education.
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to provide up to $17,500 of loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan and William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan programs

Document 4: authorizes a grant program to enable elementary schools, secondary schools, or specified entities that serve low-performing schools
to improve the academic achievement of elementary and secondary school students.
Document 5: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 by allowing a Federal Pell Grant to be awarded to a student who is dually or concurrently enrolled

Document 6: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to provide for the use of data from the second preceding tax year
Increases the limit on the adjusted gross income that parents may make if those students are to be considered as having zero expected family contribution.
Document 7: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid
makes eligible for Direct Loan program and Federal Perkins Loan program participation an educational program that provides at least 250 hours of instruction.
Document 8: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to

Document 9: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make demonstration grants

7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain decorative outdoor torches.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving health care access and services for veterans in the United States.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to place the veteran in a state licensed or certified residential care facility
if the cost of providing such care through the facility is less than the cost of providing the care through any other available option.
Document 2: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation
as service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war.
Document 3: Reduces from three years to one year the period during which a disabled veterans' outreach program specialist must complete the training program.
provided by the National Veterans' Employment and Training Services Institute.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional for continuing professional education expenses
up to $1,600 per year.
Document 5: Eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility
to veterans who are active participants in the VA's health care system.
Document 6: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to enter into contracts or agreements with eligible providers to furnish hospital care and medical services
to electing, eligible veterans.
Document 7: Includes within a program of individualized rehabilitation and reintegration plans for veterans with traumatic brain injury (TBI)
the goal of maximizing the individual's independence and quality of life.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA), in the case of any claim for veterans' disability compensation that is not adjudicated within 125 days
to pay such claimant provisional benefits.
Document 9: authorizes the city of Aurora, Colorado, to convey by donation to the United States nonfederal lands
for construction of a veterans' medical facility.
Document 10: provides that if a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical facility that would otherwise provide covered medical services
is unable to provide such services for at least 180 days due to such disaster.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Development Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, leasing, and production of oil and natural gas resources in the United States, particularly in federal lands and offshore areas.

Document 1: National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources to the United States.
mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR to include at least one lease sale annually.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands.
Establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act.
Document 3: Requires the President to initiate consultations with Indonesia, Kuwait, Nigeria, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Venezuela, Ecuador, and Saudi Arabia to seek their elimination of any action that limits the production or distribution of oil, natural gas, or any other petroleum product.
Document 4: Establishes in the Treasury the American-Made Energy Trust Fund for alternative and renewable energy incentives and projects.
Repeals the prohibition against production or leasing of oil and gas resources from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR).
Document 5: direct the Department of the Interior to make available for leasing, and conduct lease sales including, at least 50% of the available unleased acreage within each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning area.
Interior must also make available for leasing under each five-year oil and gas leasing program any OCS planning area in the Gulf of Mexico estimated to contain more than 2.5 billion barrels of oil.
Document 6: revises the policy concerning resource development on the outer Continental Shelf (OCS) to include emphasis of the importance of protection, maintenance, and restoration of the marine and coastal environments.
Authorizes the leasing, exploration, and development of oil and gas resources of the OCS only if the Secretary of the Interior determines, on the basis of sound science, that risks are minimal.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands.
Instructs the Secretary to promulgate diligent development regulations.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct lease sale 220 within one year after enactment of this Act.
Prohibits any oil or natural gas exploration, development, or production off the Virginia coast that would conflict with a military operation.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management employee to be subject to random testing for the prohibited use of a controlled substance.
Conduct monthly inspections of each vessel and facility used for Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) oil or gas drilling.
Document 10: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock.

topic.name: Immigration and Visa Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments related to immigration, nonimmigrant visas, and the legal status of aliens in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category an alien coming temporarily to the United States to work as a sheepherder or dairy worker.
Directs the Secretary of Labor to issue housing regulations for workers engaged in the range production of livestock.
Document 2: Revises the H-2A nonimmigrant agricultural worker visa program, including provisions regarding agricultural labor and services, application requirements, and housing.
Includes provisions for legal assistance and violations.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State to stop granting immigrant visas or nonimmigrant visas to citizens of a country that delays accepting an alien.
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to deny admission to citizens of such countries.
Document 5: Excludes from the definition of 'unauthorized alien' any alien lawfully present in the United States.
Considers an alien without lawful status to be an unauthorized alien whose employment is unlawful.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the detention of an alien who is deportable for failing to maintain nonimmigrant status.
ICE shall make reasonable efforts to obtain information about the identity of any victims of the crimes for which such alien was charged.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: This topic focuses on the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' programs related to electromagnetic pulse weapons and their delivery systems.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health services and interventions across various populations, including veterans, youth, and older adults.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Defense and the Department of Veterans Affairs to ensure that all health care providers... receive... training on the recognition and assessment of individuals at risk for suicide.
Requires such Departments to conduct annual evaluations of the implementation by mental health care providers...
Document 2: Authorizes the Director of the National Institutes of Health to provide for the establishment of a Youth Mental Health Research Network...
Requires the Director to ensure that an appropriate number of awards go to entities that agree to focus primarily on the early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people.
Document 3: Require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities for projects to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults...
Require the inclusion of representatives of older adults and their families and geriatric mental health professionals on the Advisory Council for the Center.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary to develop and carry out a comprehensive program designed to reduce the incidence of suicide among veterans.
Requires the program to include: mandatory training for appropriate staff and contractors of the Department of Veterans Affairs who interact with veterans.
Document 5: Reauthorizes appropriations for research, training, and technical assistance centers related to mental health, substance abuse, and the justice system and suicide.
Reauthorizes appropriations for grants to institutions of higher education for services to students with mental and behavioral health problems.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services... to award grants to eligible institutions of higher education to improve mental and behavioral health services on college campuses.
Directs the Secretary to give special consideration to programs that demonstrate the greatest need.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services... to award grants for demonstration projects to provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses...

Directs the Secretary to implement a plan for ensuring that various components of the National Health Information Infrastructure address the needs of mental health and substance abuse treatment providers.
Document 8: Requires the HHS Secretary to provide for evaluations to determine the effects of navigator services.
Directs the HHS Secretary... to award grants to states and local governments to train first responders in providing assistance to such individuals in emergency situations.
Document 9: Require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities for projects to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults...

Document 10: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to establish a five-year grant program to improve the monitoring of VA mental health and substance abuse treatment programs.

topic.name: Mortgage Foreclosure Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at preventing mortgage foreclosures and protecting homeowners through various reforms and regulations.

Document 1: Prohibits commencement of any foreclosure in connection with certain residential mortgages unless the person commencing the foreclosure complies with specified prerequisites
Requires the person commencing the foreclosure to notify the mortgagor, in writing, not less than five days before any action is taken to commence foreclosure
Document 2: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 with respect to the exercise of options on mortgage-backed securities

Defines mortgage-backed security as debt obligations issued in the form of participation certificates, collateralized mortgage obligations, mortgage-backed commercial paper, and real estate mortgage investment conduits
Document 3: Modifies the eligibility criteria for insurance of home equity conversion mortgages (reverse mortgages) of elderly homeowners
Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to use a portion of reverse mortgage insurance premiums to fund mandatory counseling and disclosure activities
Document 4: Limits the funds that may be provided to the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac)
Part of the Amended and Restated Senior Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement
Document 5: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor to exclude from the computation of debts the secured or unsecured portions of debts secured by the debtor's principal residence
Allows such a debtor to satisfy the requirement within 30 days after filing a petition for relief from debt
Document 6: Prohibits a servicer from initiating a foreclosure action against a borrower unless the servicer has made a reasonable effort to initiate mediation proceedings
Requires a servicer to suspend a foreclosure action initiated before the enactment of this Act until the servicer completes such a mediation proceeding
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to implement a competitive grants program for states and local governmental entities to establish mediation programs
Requires the program to provide the mortgagor with a referral and contact information for a paid or pro bono attorney or a HUD-certified housing counselor
Document 8: Prohibits the holder of a claim in bankruptcy secured by the debtor's principal residence from adding fees, costs, or charges while the case is pending
Authorizes a chapter 13 bankruptcy plan to provide for payment of allowed claims secured by the debtor's principal residence over a period that exceeds a specified time frame
Document 9: Excludes from computation of debts the secured or unsecured portions of debts secured by the debtor's principal residence
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders, in the event of a foreclosure notice for a chapter 13 debtor
Document 10: Directs the Chairperson of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to establish a systematic foreclosure prevention and mortgage modification program
Paying mortgage servicers $1,000 to cover expenses for each loan modified according to specified standards

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation, restoration, and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.

Authorizes the Secretary, in cooperation with the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), to provide assistance to any state, local, or territorial government agency for any unforeseen or disaster-related circumstance regarding coral reef ecosystems.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to execute contracts, leases, grants, or cooperative agreements as necessary to carry out the Act, including entering into agreements with universities and research centers.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress and may establish an international coral reef ecosystem partnership program.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts, derelict fishing gear, vessel anchors and anchor chains.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function from vessel impacts.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 8: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, and education necessary to protect and restore coral reefs.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 10: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the enforcement of penalties for breaches of data privacy.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.

Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record if such information includes the name, social security number, street address, telephone number, or electronic mail address.
Document 2: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Imposes a prison term of up to five years and/or a fine on any individual who has knowledge of and intentionally and willfully conceals a security breach.
Document 4: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

Makes it unlawful for a service provider to knowingly or intentionally redirect web searches or otherwise monitor, manipulate, aggregate, and market data from websites without the consent of the Internet user.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to provide criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Defines 'security breach' as a compromise of the security, confidentiality, or integrity of computerized data.
